segments.text: First listen, and then answer the question.

translation.text: 先听录音，然后回答问题。

segments.text: The great racing driver, Sir Malcolm Campbell, was the first man to drive at over 300 miles per hour.

translation.text: 伟大的赛车手马尔科姆·坎贝尔爵士是世界上第一个以超过每小时300英里的速度驾车的人。

segments.text: He set up a new world record in September 1935 at Bonneville Salt Flats, Utah.

translation.text: 1935年9月，他在美国犹他州博纳维尔盐滩创造了新的世界纪录。

segments.text: Bluebird, the car he was driving, had been specially built for him.

translation.text: 他驾驶的“蓝鸟”是专门为他制造的。

segments.text: It was over 30 feet in length and had a 2,500 horsepower engine.

translation.text: 车身长30多英尺，装有一台2,500马力的发动机。

segments.text: Although Campbell reached a speed of over 304 miles per hour, he had great difficulty in controlling the car because a tire burst during the first run.

translation.text: 尽管坎贝尔的时速超过了304英里，他却很难控制汽车，因为第一次行驶时一只轮胎爆裂了。

segments.text: After his attempt, Campbell was disappointed to learn that his average speed had been 299 miles per hour.

translation.text: 尝试结束后，坎贝尔得知平均时速只有299英里，感到很失望。

segments.text: However, a few days later, he was told that a mistake had been made.

translation.text: 然而几天后，有人告诉他计算出了差错。

segments.text: His average speed had been 301 miles per hour.

translation.text: 他的平均时速其实是301英里。

segments.text: Since that time, racing drivers have reached speeds over 600 miles per hour.

translation.text: 从那以后，赛车手达到的速度已经超过每小时600英里。

segments.text: Following in his father's footsteps many years later, Sir Malcolm's son, Donald, set up a world record.

translation.text: 多年以后，马尔科姆爵士的儿子唐纳德继承父业，也创造了一项世界纪录。

segments.text: Like his father, he was driving a car called Bluebird.

translation.text: 和父亲一样，他驾驶的汽车也叫“蓝鸟”。
